Walk me through why my recursive Fibonacci is so slow and how memoization would fix it.
Build a token bucket rate limiter in Go that supports per-client limits and burst capacity, with thread-safe refill logic.
Write a recursive descent parser for a small arithmetic grammar that handles parentheses, operator precedence, and unary minus.
Implement an LRU cache with O(1) get and put using a hash map plus doubly linked list, including eviction.
I need a retry helper with exponential backoff and jitter that respects a max attempts cap and a deadline. Walk me through the implementation.
Design a connection pool for a database client that handles idle timeouts, max connections, and graceful checkout under contention.
Create a debounce and throttle utility pair in TypeScript with proper cleanup, leading and trailing edge options.
Write a function that merges k sorted linked lists efficiently and explain the time complexity tradeoff you chose.
Implement a sliding window log-based rate counter that gives accurate per-minute request counts without storing every timestamp.
Build a small event-driven state machine for an order lifecycle: pending, paid, shipped, cancelled, with guarded transitions.
Design a circuit breaker that trips after a failure threshold, half-opens after a cooldown, and tracks rolling error rates.
Help me write a Trie that supports insert, search, prefix matching, and wildcard single-character queries.
Implement a job scheduler that runs tasks at fixed intervals, handles overlapping runs, and supports pause and resume per task.
I want a streaming CSV parser that handles quoted fields, embedded newlines, and configurable delimiters without loading the whole file.
Write a binary serialization layer for a struct with versioning so older readers can skip unknown fields gracefully.
Build a priority queue backed by a binary heap and use it to implement Dijkstra shortest path on a weighted graph.
Our checkout latency spiked from 200ms to 1.4s after the last deploy and I'm not sure if it's the new caching layer or the database connection pool. Walk me through how to isolate the cause.
I have ten years of monthly revenue, churn, and ad-spend data for three product lines. Figure out which channels actually drive retention versus just acquisition and how confident we can be.
Redesign our image-upload pipeline so it handles 50MB files, retries on failure, deduplicates, and stays under our current memory budget. Lay out the tradeoffs.
Compare adopting a monorepo against keeping our twelve separate repos given our team size, CI costs, and how often changes span services, then recommend a path.
We need to migrate authentication from session cookies to JWTs without logging everyone out and without breaking the mobile apps mid-rollout. How should we sequence it?
Profile why this report-generation job uses 8GB of RAM and takes four minutes, then propose specific changes ranked by effort versus payoff.
Plan a phased rewrite of our legacy billing engine into the new service while both run in parallel and reconcile nightly so finance never sees a discrepancy.
Given conflicting requirements from sales (fast onboarding) and security (strict approvals), design an account-provisioning flow that satisfies both and explain the compromises.
Help me restructure this 4000-word strategy memo so the argument builds logically, the evidence supports each claim, and the recommendations don't contradict the risks section.
We're seeing intermittent data corruption when two services write to the same record. Diagnose the likely race conditions and lay out fixes with their consistency tradeoffs.
Model the cash-flow impact of switching from annual to monthly billing across our customer base, accounting for churn changes, deferred revenue, and collection timing.
Refactor our error-handling so every service reports failures consistently, retries are bounded, and we stop swallowing exceptions, without rewriting business logic everywhere.
Design a feature-flag rollout strategy for shipping the new search backend to 5% then 100% of users while keeping a clean rollback path and measuring impact.
Audit our Kubernetes setup for why pods get evicted under load and recommend resource limits, autoscaling rules, and scheduling changes with the reasoning behind each.
I need to consolidate three overlapping customer datasets with mismatched IDs and date formats into one clean source of truth, deciding how to resolve conflicts.
Design a globally distributed ledger that guarantees strict serializability across five regions while surviving any single region going dark, and walk me through the consensus and clock-sync choices.

We need to migrate a 40-billion-row monolithic Postgres into a sharded multi-tenant fleet with zero downtime, online resharding, and consistent cross-shard transactions. How would you stage this end to end?

Architect a real-time fraud detection platform processing two million events per second with sub-100ms decisions, exactly-once semantics, and a feedback loop that retrains models without disrupting live scoring.

Help me think through a multi-region active-active CQRS system where writes can originate anywhere, conflicts must auto-resolve deterministically, and read replicas stay within a bounded staleness window.
Lay out a complete plan to rewrite our payments core from a synchronous monolith into an event-sourced saga-based architecture while keeping reconciliation provably correct during the cutover.

I want to build a planet-scale object store with erasure coding, self-healing on disk failure, tunable consistency per bucket, and predictable tail latency under correlated failures. Where do I start?
Reason through how to shard and replicate a social graph of a billion users so that friend-of-friend queries stay fast, hot nodes don't melt, and a datacenter loss doesn't lose edges.

Propose an end-to-end strategy for hedging a multi-currency derivatives book in real time given correlated market shocks, liquidity constraints, regulatory capital rules, and conflicting desk objectives.

Design a federated learning system across thousands of hospitals that preserves differential privacy, tolerates dropout and stragglers, defends against poisoning, and still converges on a useful model.
Work out the full failover and quorum design for a metadata service that must never lose a committed write, recover automatically from split-brain, and keep latency stable during leader churn.

How would you build a deterministic, reproducible build-and-deploy pipeline for a thousand-microservice estate with progressive rollout, automatic rollback on SLO breach, and cross-service version compatibility guarantees?
Plan a research-grade evaluation of three competing consensus protocols under partition, clock skew, and Byzantine nodes, then synthesize which to adopt given our latency and durability targets.

Help me redesign our streaming data platform so that backfills, late-arriving events, schema evolution, and exactly-once sinks all coexist without ever producing duplicate or dropped records downstream.
